stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Current trout fishing quality on the Soque River is fair to good with a flow of 64 cfs and moderate gage height at 3.55 ft. Recent stocking supports good action on egg patterns, squirmy worms, and attractor nymphs.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 1 morning due to low wind speeds and moderate sky cover, providing comfortable conditions for trout to feed near cover and for anglers to achieve clean drifts with natural and stocked-trout attractor flies.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is unavailable. Flow is 64.0 cfs, gage height is 3.55 ft, and turbidity data is unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages around 20%, pressure data is unavailable, and there is a slight chance of rain at 10% with no expected accumulation. Winds average 2.2 mph from the east with gusts up to 11.5 mph.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will likely feed in the most comfortable lanes close to cover under lighter sky conditions. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell nymphs to entice bites. No caution notes are indicated.
